concerns.text: User Story 1 requires 'weekly frequency' alignment for GDP, unemployment, and sentiment. GDP from FRED is quarterly; unemployment is monthly. Interpolating GDP to weekly introduces artificial precision that violates time-series integrity principles in the constitution (VI).

Granger causality tests require sufficient time series length for statistical power. With only 2 recession periods (2008, 2020) totaling ~30 months combined, VAR lag selection and causality tests may lack power to detect meaningful relationships. This is a fundamental design limitation.
FR-007 is malformed: 'System MUST validate results across The analysis will focus on...' contains incomplete requirement text. The validation target definition is unclear and untestable as written.

Bootstrap validation 'across specific recession periods' conflates re-sampling with external validation. If bootstrapping uses the same recession data it's meant to validate, this is circular. Need independent test periods or out-of-sample validation design.
FR-006 example requirement should be removed from the requirements section. It's an implementation detail (Jupyter Notebook format) not a functional requirement for the statistical analysis itself.
